chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Constitution Petition No.S-1418 of 2024 ____________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) Hearing/Priority 1. For orders on office objection No.5 and reply as at “A”. 2. For hearing of CMA No.545/2025 (U/O-I Rule-10 CPC). 3. For hearing of CMA No.10718/2024 (U/A-199 of Const.) 4. For hearing of main case. -.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- 02.06.2025 Ms. Summaya, advocate holding brief for Malik Khushhal Khan, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Zia-ul-Haq Makhdoom, advocate for Intervener. -.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Ms. Summaya, advocate holding brief for Malik Khushhal Khan, advocate for the petitioner and states that the latter is unwell. The petitioner has sought indulgence of this Court against the officials of the police department, as they are harassing him. Perusal of the contents of the petition reveals that there is a dispute between the private parties who are not part of the proceedings in the instant lis and an application under Order-I Rule-10 CPC has been filed by the said intervener to implead him as a party. Though from the perusal of the petition the disputed questions of facts have been agitated, which is not the domain of this Court to interfere or to issue any direction with regard to those controversies, however, official respondents i.e., SSP Malir, Karachi and SHO P.S Shah Latif Town, Karachi are directed to conduct themselves strictly in accordance with law and not to cause any harassment to the petitioner. As far as the prayer clause “a” is concerned, the petitioner is seeking certain restraint orders with respect to the Plots No.A, B, C, I and J at Jumma Goth for agriculture purpose for which he

Though from the perusal of the petition the disputed questions of facts have been agitated, which is not the domain of this Court to interfere or to issue any direction with regard to those controversies, however, official respondents i.e., SSP Malir, Karachi and SHO P.S Shah Latif Town, Karachi are directed to conduct themselves strictly in accordance with law and not to cause any harassment to the petitioner. As far as the prayer clause “a” is concerned, the petitioner is seeking certain restraint orders with respect to the Plots No.A, B, C, I and J at Jumma Goth for agriculture purpose for which he may avail the remedy before the Civil Court and this Court cannot entertain such issues in its writ jurisdiction. With these observations, this petition stands disposed of along with pending applications. JUDGE Ayaz Gul
